text:
Engineering Graphics and CAD  
Subject Code - FCME0116 
 
SHEET NO.-3                                      TITLE: PROJECTIONS OF POINTS 
 
Question 1: 
A point is 25 mm above the HP and 30 mm in front of the VP. Draw its projections. 
 
Question 2: 
A point  is  15  mm  above  the  HP  and  20  mm  in  front  of  the  VP.  Another point  Q  is  25  mm 
behind  the  VP  and  40  mm  below  the  HP.  Draw  projection  of  P  and  Q  keeping  the  distance 
between their  projectors  equal  to  90  mm.  Draw  straight lines  joining  (i)  their top  views  and 
(ii) their front views. 
 
Question 3: 
A point P is 20 mm below the HP and lies in the third quadrant. Its shortest distance from XY 
is 40 mm. Draw its projections. 
 
Question 4: 
A point A is situated in the first quadrant. Its shortest distance from the intersection point of 
HP, VP and auxiliary plane is 60 mm and it is equidistant from the principal planes. Draw the 
projections of the point and determine its distance from the principal planes. 
 
Question 5: 
A point 30 mm above the XY line is the plan-view of the two points P and Q. The elevation 
of  P  is  45  mm  above  the  HP  while  that  of  the  point  Q  is  35  mm  below  the  HP.  Draw  the 
projections  of  the  points  and  states  their  position  with  reference  to  the  principal  planes  and 
the quadrants in which they lie. 
 
Question 6: 
A point Q is situated in the first quadrant. It is 40 mm above the HP and 30 mm in front of 
VP,  Draw  its  projections  and  find  its  shortest  distance  from  the  intersection  of  HP,  VP  and 
auxiliary plane.
